page: Amazon Web Services r7iz.metal-16xl is a memory-optimized bare metal server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6455B processors. It features 64 vCPUs across 32 physical cores with a base clock speed of 3.9 GHz, supported by 60 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in several areas. The server achieves top-tier (top 10%) results in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, single-threaded bzip3 decompression, and PassMark database operations. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks rank in the top 25%. For machine learning workloads, LLM inference prompt processing with a 7B model reaches top-tier speeds, while text generation for a 70B model falls into the average category.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r7iz.metal-16xl offers high resource density and memory bandwidth, making it highly efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. However, the lack of bundled local storage and GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, requiring external storage solutions and limiting its utility for heavy GPU-accelerated tasks.

This server is optimized for memory-intensive and high-compute workloads. It is highly suitable for large-scale database operations, memory-bound data processing, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.metal-16xl is a memory-optimized bare metal server featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6455B processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The server delivers top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core benchmarks, as well as PassMark database operations. It also shows strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth. While highly efficient for memory-intensive applications, the absence of local storage requires network-attached alternatives. This server is best suited for large-scale database operations, memory-bound data processing, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.metal-16xl is a memory-optimized bare metal server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering top-tier database and multi-core performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized bare metal server featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for demanding database and compute workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs via Intel Xeon 6455B processors running at 3.9 GHz
512.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier performance in database operations and Geekbench benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for large-scale databases, software compilation, and LLM inference
